title: Softmax-recip HBM-closed on-chip schedule closure for Llama7B attention
hypothesis: After replacing the inherited scalar HBM assumption with the measured softmax-recip HBM service model, re-sweeping explicit SRAM and NoC service knobs should show whether the current frontier is still tile-attention limited before scheduling subtile QK/softmax/V pipelining.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does re-optimizing on-chip SRAM and NoC service knobs on top of the softmax-recip measured-HBM Llama7B frontier change latency or the dominant resource?
include: Sweep schedule policy, bank arbitration, endpoint queue depth, bank queue depth, router hop latency, packet payload, and prefetch overlap.
Keep measured softmax reciprocal LUT costs, measured SRAM rebalance quantities, measured HBM controller service cycles, compute PPA, and KV sharing assumptions inherited from the source frontier.
Report best latency, slowdown versus the measured-HBM source, dominant resource, queue/router settings, HBM cycles, attention cycles, and shared-service cycles.
exclude: Do not introduce a new full NoC RTL implementation in this item.
Do not change HBM channel, burst, row-hit, or outstanding-request parameters.
Do not change SRAM macro packing, compute replica count, precision, or KV sharing quality assumptions.
Do not evaluate subtile QK/softmax/V pipelining until this HBM-closed on-chip source artifact is materialized.
follow_on_broad_sweep: If tile attention remains dominant, run the softmax-recip subtile pipeline schedule item using this artifact as its source.
If NoC or SRAM service becomes dominant, split out concrete endpoint/router/topology RTL and scheduler variants before further compute-path tuning.
If HBM service becomes dominant, move to a fuller DRAM timing and controller area/power model.

expected_benefit: Removes inherited on-chip schedule bias from the current softmax-recip measured-HBM frontier.
Confirms whether the measured-HBM closure still leaves room in the tile-attention path.
Creates the required materialized source artifact for the dependent subtile pipeline schedule exploration.
risks: The on-chip service model is still analytic rather than a fully routed NoC implementation.
Queue and prefetch combinations may be optimistic until translated into ready/valid RTL.
HBM controller area and power remain modeled analytically rather than measured as placed logic.

expected_result.reason: The result should record whether re-sweeping on-chip SRAM/NoC service knobs changes the current softmax-recip measured-HBM Llama7B attention frontier before subtile pipelining.
